input.external_goal: Catch person responsible for staged suicides
input.surface_description: Competent, respected, calm under pressure. Keeps everyone at arm's length.

test_cases.notes: Wound is specific and scene-ready. Want/need clearly distinct and in dramatic tension. Voice signature includes concrete micro-dialogue. Ghost is active. Arc differentiated clearly from starting point.

input.role: Antagonist — a mid-level pharmaceutical sales director who knowingly suppressed adverse trial data
input.external_goal: Protect the drug launch and his own career
input.surface_description: Charming, family man, coached his kid's soccer team for six years
input.tone: Systemic villain, not a monster. References: Dopesick, Margin Call.

test_cases.notes: Excellent handling of systemic antagonist without moralizing or flattening. Consistency docked: one biographical detail implied a specific corporate structure not present in the input.

input.role: Supporting character — protagonist's estranged adult daughter who resurfaces mid-story
input.external_goal: Get her mother to acknowledge what her childhood was actually like
input.surface_description: Competent, contained, married with one child. Sounds like her mother.
input.tone: Quiet, devastating. References: Ordinary People, We Need to Talk About Kevin.

test_cases.notes: Solid profile for a supporting character functioning as mirror. All five sections present and appropriately scaled. Voice signature micro-dialogue slightly generic. Ghost section felt more thematic than scene-ready.
